metadata.title: MailKit — Email on your Cloudflare domain in under 10 minutes
metadata.description: Already on Cloudflare DNS? Automated Email Routing + Brevo SMTP setup with guided Gmail Send-As. $5 per mailbox.

announcementBanner.message: 🎁 First 100 setups — FREE with code FIRST100.

hero.subhead: Already on Cloudflare DNS? We configure Email Routing and Brevo SMTP for you, then walk you through Gmail Send-As — three copy-paste actions, done.
hero.microcopy: Cloudflare DNS required. Not on Cloudflare? Free migration takes ~30 min.
hero.timeSplit: 10 minutes of your time. The rest is on us.
hero.trustNote: Free during promo · No subscription required

mockup.alt: Gmail Compose window — the From dropdown shows your custom-domain addresses instead of @gmail.com

pricing.heading: Five dollars. One time. Done.
pricing.subhead: No subscription. No recurring fee. No surprise charges next month.

Unlimited extra addresses on your domain (no extra charge)

howItWorks.heading: Four steps. We do three. You do one.
howItWorks.subheading: Total time under 10 minutes. Total work on your side: three copy-paste actions in Gmail.

step1.title: Cloudflare Email Routing
step1.body: We turn on email routing for your domain and add the records that let it receive mail.

step2.title: Brevo SMTP authentication
step2.body: We register your domain in our Brevo account and add the signature records that prove your email is yours.

step3.title: DNS verification
step3.body: We check the records are live and Gmail will accept email signed by your domain.

step4.title: Gmail Send-As
step4.body: We hand you four lines — server, port, login, password — and walk you through pasting them into Gmail. Gmail then sends a confirmation link, you click it, done.

problem.heading: Setting up email on your own domain takes about an hour.
problem.subhead: Finding a current guide, navigating three different dashboards, and knowing which field gets what — that's where most people get stuck.

without.title: Manual setup
steps: Find an up-to-date guide on email routing
Create accounts and figure out multiple service dashboards
Copy and paste the right credentials into the right fields
Set up Gmail Send-As without mistakes
Hit a wall at any point and start over

with.title: Automated
with.tokenHint: How to create your API token in Cloudflare dashboard

trust.heading: We refund first, ask questions later.
trust.subhead: Two clear guarantees, written in plain language.

automation.title: Automation failure
automation.body: If our automated setup fails on our end (Cloudflare or Brevo phase) — full refund within 24 hours. Automatic. No request needed.

functional.title: 30-day functional
functional.body: If within 30 days you can't actually send email through your domain — even after our support — full refund on request. Just email us.

trust.audienceNote: Built for Cloudflare DNS users. Free migration guide for users on other registrars.
objections: Don't know DNS, SMTP, or DKIM? You don't need to — we do.
Worried about breaking something? We offer a full money-back guarantee.
Don't want to spend your evening on guides and forums? We do it for you.

items.q: Does this work with any domain?
items.a: Right now MailKit only works if your domain uses Cloudflare DNS. If your domain is on GoDaddy, Namecheap, Squarespace, or another registrar, you'll need to migrate nameservers to Cloudflare first. Cloudflare DNS is free and migration takes about 30 minutes plus DNS propagation up to 24 hours. After migration, MailKit setup runs in under 10 minutes. Multi-provider support is on our roadmap.

Your domain, DNS records, Cloudflare Email Routing, and Gmail Send-As are yours forever — they live on your accounts, not ours. The only piece tied to our infrastructure is the SMTP relay (AWS SES). If we ever close, we give you 90 days notice plus a step-by-step guide to migrate the SMTP relay to your own AWS SES or alternative provider account. No lock-in.
10 minutes of active work, then 5-30 minutes while Amazon verifies your domain's DNS. We'll email you when it's ready. Then just 3 minutes to paste four lines into Gmail.

items.q: What if I have no technical skills?
items.a: That's exactly what we're here for. DNS, SMTP, DKIM, DMARC — that's our part. You paste four lines into Gmail following our step-by-step guide. No technical configuration required.
No. Email configuration doesn't touch your website settings. Email and web run on different DNS records — we only modify the ones needed for email.

items.q: Do I have to keep paying every month?
items.a: No. Mailkit is a one-time $5 fee per mailbox — no subscription, no recurring charges. That covers the full automated setup: Cloudflare Email Routing for receiving, Brevo SMTP for sending, and the step-by-step Gmail Send-As walkthrough. Compared to Google Workspace at $6/user/month ($72 a year per mailbox), Mailkit is a one-and-done payment.

items.q: Can I do this myself for free?
items.a: Yes — by hand it takes 60–90 minutes for an experienced engineer and 3+ hours for everyone else, juggling Cloudflare Email Routing, Brevo SMTP, five DNS records (MX, SPF, DKIM signature, DMARC policy, Brevo verification) and the Gmail Send-As setup. Mailkit collapses that into under 10 minutes for $5: ~15 sec for the Cloudflare phase, ~60 sec for Brevo, ~3 min of copy-paste in Gmail.

items.q: How is this different from Google Workspace?
items.a: Google Workspace gives you a separate inbox at $6/user/month forever — a new mailbox to check, a separate password, full migration if you stop paying. Mailkit configures your existing Gmail to send and receive from your domain via Gmail Send-As. Same Gmail inbox, no migration, no monthly fee. The trade-off: with Mailkit your domain email lives inside your personal Gmail, not in a Workspace tenant.

items.q: What happens if the setup fails halfway?
items.a: If our Cloudflare or Brevo automation fails, we issue an automatic full refund within 24 hours. The dashboard shows you which step failed and why. If you'd like to retry once we've fixed our side, we'll let you know — no extra charge if the failure was on our end.

items.q: Does it work with my domain registered at Namecheap, GoDaddy, or other registrars?
items.a: MailKit currently requires Cloudflare DNS. If your domain is on GoDaddy, Namecheap, Squarespace or another registrar, you need to migrate nameservers to Cloudflare first (free, about 30 minutes plus DNS propagation). After migration, MailKit setup runs in under 10 minutes.

items.q: Why doesn't Mailkit fully automate the Gmail step?
items.a: The Gmail API method that adds Send-As entries (gmail.settings.sendAs.create) is blocked on personal @gmail.com accounts — it requires Google Workspace with domain-wide delegation. We made that part a 3-minute guided walkthrough with copy-paste values instead. The other technical parts (Cloudflare + Brevo + DNS) are fully automated.

items.q: Is my Cloudflare API token safe with you?
items.a: We use the token only during the active setup session and discard it from memory afterwards. We do not persist it in our database. The token can be scoped to the minimum permissions our wizard documents (Zone:DNS:Edit + Email Routing edits on the specific zone) and can be revoked from your Cloudflare dashboard at any time.

items.q: Why might my emails go to spam?
items.a: Mailkit configures technically correct SPF policy, DKIM signature, and DMARC authentication. Whether email lands in a specific recipient's inbox depends on sender reputation, content, sending practices, and the recipient's policies — all outside Mailkit's control. Domain warmup, recipient list hygiene, and consent compliance are the sender's job. Practical advice: send 10–20 emails per day in the first week from a fresh domain, then ramp up gradually.

items.q: How is this different from ImprovMX?
items.a: Mailkit replaces the receiving piece (ImprovMX forwarding) with Cloudflare Email Routing and adds outbound sending via Brevo SMTP through Gmail Send-As — ImprovMX's free tier doesn't do outbound. You'll need to remove the existing MX records on your domain before setup; our wizard detects conflicts and tells you exactly which records block the run.

items.q: Do you support Google Workspace mailboxes?
items.a: Not at launch. The Gmail Send-As walkthrough is built for personal @gmail.com accounts — Workspace admins can already create domain mailboxes natively without Mailkit. If there's enough demand, Workspace support is on the post-launch roadmap.

finalCta.heading: Under 10 minutes from now, you'll have email on your Cloudflare domain.
finalCta.subhead: Or your money back, automatically. No tickets, no calls, no questions.

dashboard.title: Welcome back, {name}.
dashboard.subtitle: Manage your domains, view past setups, contact support, or buy another mailbox.

emptyState.title: Set up your first domain
emptyState.body: You haven't started a setup yet. It takes under 10 minutes — paste a Cloudflare API token, pick your zone, follow the Gmail wizard.

deleteConfirm.title: Delete your MailKit account?
deleteConfirm.body: This removes your profile, setup runs, and purchase records from MailKit. Your domain's DNS records and Gmail Send-As config stay where they are — those live in Cloudflare and Gmail. This can't be undone.

nsWarning.heading: {domain} doesn't appear to use Cloudflare DNS
nsWarning.body: MailKit currently requires Cloudflare DNS. Migrate your nameservers to Cloudflare first (free, about 30 minutes). After migration, run setup again.

setup.title: Connect your domain
setup.subtitle: Three steps: paste your Cloudflare token, pick the zone, watch us wire it up.

step1.title: Step 1 — Paste your Cloudflare API token
step1.tokenHelp: Create at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens — template Custom token, scopes: Zone:Zone:Read, Zone:DNS:Edit, Zone:Zone Settings:Edit, Zone:Email Routing Rules:Edit (all four zone-scoped; set Zone Resource to the specific zone) + Account:Email Routing Addresses:Edit (account-scoped).

step2.title: Step 2 — Pick the zone and mailbox
step2.mailboxHint: This becomes {local}@your-domain — lowercase letters, digits, dot, dash, underscore.

awaitingVerify.title: Check your Gmail inbox
awaitingVerify.body: Cloudflare sent a verification link to {email}. Click it, then retry.

done.title: Cloudflare is ready
done.body: Your domain now routes {mailbox}@{domain} to Gmail. Next up: Brevo SMTP — coming in Ticket #4b.

failed.title: Setup failed
failed.body: We couldn't finish the Cloudflare step. Fix the issue below and restart from Step 1.
failed.bodyBrevo: We couldn't finish the Brevo step. Fix the issue below and restart from Step 1.

errors.invalid_token: The API token is invalid or missing required permissions.
errors.zone_not_found: Zone not found in your Cloudflare account.
errors.dns_rejected: Cloudflare rejected a DNS record. Details: {details}
errors.rate_limited: Too many requests. Try again in a minute.
errors.cloudflare_unavailable: Cloudflare is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.no_primary_email: Your Google account email is missing — sign out and sign back in.
errors.run_wrong_state: This setup run is not in the right state for this action.
errors.run_corrupt: Setup run is missing required data; start a new one.
errors.db_insert_failed: Could not record the setup run. Try again.
errors.brevo_invalid_token: Brevo rejected our API key. Hold tight while we rotate it — nothing for you to do.
errors.brevo_domain_taken: Brevo already knows this domain from a previous run — we picked up the existing setup.
errors.brevo_rate_limited: Brevo throttled our requests. Retrying shortly.
errors.brevo_unavailable: Brevo is temporarily unavailable. Try again in a few minutes.
errors.brevo_verify_timeout: Brevo could not verify your domain yet. DNS propagation can take a few minutes — retry soon.
errors.brevo_state_unrecoverable: Brevo knows this domain but did not return the DKIM records we need to configure DNS. The existing Brevo entry is in a partial state — please ping support.
errors.spf_conflict: Your SPF record ends in -all (strict policy). Soften it to ~all so Brevo can send on your behalf, then retry.
errors.dns_write_failed: We could not write a DNS record cleanly. Details: {details}
errors.brevo_smtp_misconfigured: Our SMTP relay is not configured yet. This is on us — nothing to fix on your side; ping support if you see this for more than a minute.

brevoSteps.dns: Brevo DNS records written (DKIM, brevo-code, SPF merged, DMARC)

recheck.title: Waiting for Brevo to verify DNS
recheck.body: DNS records are in place. Brevo hasn't confirmed them yet — propagation usually takes a few minutes.

terminal.title: Email infrastructure ready
terminal.body: Brevo now handles outbound mail for {mailbox}@{domain}. One step left — Gmail Send-As.

gmailStep.title: Gmail Send-As — coming soon
gmailStep.body: This step is Ticket #6. Your Cloudflare routing and Brevo SMTP are already live — you'll plug Gmail in as the last link once the UI lands.

intro.title: Last step — Gmail Send-As
intro.subtitle: About 3 minutes of copy-paste. You'll add {target} to Gmail so you can send from it like a regular address.

openSettings.title: Open Gmail → Settings
openSettings.body: In Gmail, click the ⚙ gear (top-right) → See all settings → Accounts and Import → Send mail as → Add another email address.

senderInfo.title: Fill in sender info
senderInfo.body: Paste these into the first Gmail dialog. Leave "Treat as an alias" checked.

smtpSettings.title: SMTP settings
smtpSettings.body: Gmail asks for an SMTP server next. Paste these four values. The password is your only chance to copy it — keep it safe.
smtpSettings.passwordWarning: We don't store this password for you. Copy it now; if you lose it, ping support and we'll help.

verificationEmail.title: Gmail will email a confirmation code
verificationEmail.body: Gmail sends a 9-digit code to {target}. Because Cloudflare forwards {target} to your inbox, it will arrive in the same Gmail you're using. Open the email, click the link OR paste the code back into the Gmail dialog.

confirm.title: Confirm it works
confirm.body: Open Gmail → Compose → the From field should now let you pick {target}. Send a quick test email to yourself to be sure.
confirm.checkbox: I added the address and verified it in Gmail

done.title: Done — you're sending from your domain
done.body: Compose in Gmail, switch From to {target}, send. Receipts land in your inbox as usual.
done.warmupTip: Tip: warm up the domain gradually — 10–20 emails per day in the first week, then ramp up. Bursts from a fresh domain are the fastest way to land in spam.

errors.copyFailed: Your browser blocked clipboard access. Select the value and copy it by hand.
errors.checkboxRequired: Please confirm you've added and verified the address in Gmail.

footer.tagline: Email on your domain. Setup in 10 minutes. Backed by a 30-day guarantee.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Mailkit. Built by an independent founder.

error.title: Something broke on our end
error.body: An unexpected error happened. We've logged it and we're looking into it. You can try refreshing the page or heading back home.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.body: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved. If you got here from a Mailkit email, support can help.

cookieConsent.body: We use essential cookies to make this site work.
